reviews.review_msg: MarkStrat simulation is quite fun, but a bit shallow in terms of what you might be able to learn from using case studies. Overall very high level teaching. Quite clear in his lessons, but they seem to be a bit disjointed and not around a central topic at times (the nature of strategy?). Wish that he was more organized.
Lisa is a great prof, and enthusiastic about sustainability. Highly recommend!

Extremely experienced in the real world and extremely successful. His stories give you a very unique perspective on marketing and business, as it is things that academic profs would never say. Very very interesting stories.
Expect to read 730 pages (ch.1-22) for the final exam. useless lecture, but forced to go. although no one fails in his class, but u'll suffer. trust me...sigh

Yupin was very kind and understanding. The class was easy and consisted of many group presentations and papers. Interesting cases about the industry but she doesn't teach anything outside of the case so you don't learn much from this class.

Didn't learn anything from the prof, not a single marketing theory was taught!! The class is mainly student led lecture, the prof was doing nothing. There is no grading scheme and syllabus for each assignment. The class average is superrrr high. So DO NOT TAKE 446 WITH HER!! PERIODDD!!!!!
Not much relevant real world experience.  Hypothetical and not relevant.

Best professor in a while. He talks in metaphors but he has a lot of industry experiences. He challenges you so that you can be prepared for the outside working world. He is very open to comments and keeps you thinking that there is no RIGHT answer. He is very organized gives feed back on your performances.
Excellent prof. Knows what he is talking about...will prepare you for the real world. Will be taking his B2B class next sem!
I love his BUS446 class.
Best Prof I have had at SFU, if not the best prof ever. He tells you straight up like it is. Class isnt about fancy terms, its about using your brain. This is the way uni should be taught.
Super nice guy, but he started to state questionable fact...makes you wonder if he actually knows what he is taking about.
States things like facts, but when investigated you'll find a stretched truth
He's never there at office hours. Nice guy, but not a very good teacher.
Can't teach. Never available during office hours, gives vague instructions.

I learned a lot from Robert in BUS 446 (Marketing Strategy). He is a very knowledgable prof with a good attitude toward helping students. Lectures are engaging as the majority is spent in class discussion about the weeks case reading. No exams, only projects and assignments. Not easy, but fair, marking.
Totally agree with the last person. Do not leave SFU without taking her course. She helps you to find the inner-you!
BEST PROFESSOR I HAVE EVER MET! Knowledgeable and nice person!
Best prof I've ever had, had him for b2b as well as marketing strategy. Got A+ in both!! Highly recommend, he has a very laid back way of teaching and is pretty funny
Awesome teacher, dynamic, passionate and very interesting
I have never bothered to rate a prof, But Adam is so great that i had spread his awesomeness. The best prof by far.

Not sure if everyone will appreciate this but I loved the uncensored enthusiasm/craziness. Very engaging speaker, shares cool marketing tidbits ex. quality formula, and dissects cases creatively. I found the weird/boring cases like Shouldice and Curled Metal to be the coolest actually, and I also liked the cool trading assignment. Recommended!
